1. Training Curves

Rolling average (window=500) of episode reward and win rate during training.

2. Success Rate vs. Car Count (Difficulty Curve)

Agent trained on 4 cars, then evaluated greedily across different car counts. Shows how difficulty scales — answers §3 Q1.

3. Average Episode Length vs. Car Count

Measures policy efficiency. Shorter episodes on easy configs, longer (or capped) on hard ones — answers §3 Q2.

4. Episode Length Distribution per Car Count

Box plot showing variance in episode length — reveals whether the agent is consistent or lucky.

5. Learning Evidence: Action Distribution vs Random Guessing

For key episodes (early/mid/late), compare action-choice distribution from the learned policy against a random-action baseline using the same episode seed. This directly shows non-random behavior.
